mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of observations when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained monthly decline in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals accelerating labor market tightening, which boosts consumer spending expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand recovery, which directly lifts corporate earnings forecasts and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST both rise, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation readings during a period of near-zero policy rates trigger expectations of premature monetary tightening or real rate compression, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >50% of high-inflation observations, or the negative predictive power vanishes when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Higher industrial production index values reflect stronger manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which correlate with elevated corporate profit margins and generate positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for INDPRO remains below 0.01 across all rolling 2-year windows, or positive INDPRO readings coincide with negative 20-day returns in >55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A single-month payroll employment gain exceeding 200,000 signals robust labor demand and wage growth potential, which expands aggregate consumption and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases > 200k in a single month
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative in >65% of months where PAYEMS rises above 200,000, or the threshold effect disappears in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real GDP growth combined with stable inflation indicates healthy economic expansion without pricing pressures, supporting sustained equity valuations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative 60-day returns in >50% of observations, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over individual series in cross-sectional tests.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production while unemployment remains above 7.0% indicates structural supply-side weakness and persistent labor slack, which suppresses earnings growth and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >55% of observations meeting the trigger, or the negative signal loses significance when UNRATE falls below 6.0%.
